_note: Single source of truth for creatures: spawner reads tier/hp/spawn/drops; bestiary UI reads dex.*. Kill/capture tracking lives in state.bestiary = { <id>: { kills, seen } }. A creature entry is a locked ??? box until seen=true (first defeat/capture); after that its kill count shows, and each dex.reveal.<field> is a kill threshold that unlocks that block. Optional per-creature fields: "sprite" = path to a PNG that replaces the built-in pixel art (drop the file in and reload; if it's missing the game falls back to the drawn art — a 404 in the console until you add it is harmless), "spriteSize" = draw size in px (default 44), "enabled": false = fully removed from spawning + the bestiary (a clean way to shelf a creature without deleting it). Each entry in the "resources" map supports the same "sprite"/"spriteSize" override for its dropped-material icon (default drawn from built-in letterless pixel icons).

dex.description: A gelatinous cube of congealed, unwritten ideas. It doesn't bite — it just wobbles toward you and gets in the way until you're pinned against it.
dex.lore: Every writer meets one eventually. Break it down and it yields something useful — usually cheese, occasionally glue. Nobody asks why cheese.

dex.description: A plump grub that tunnels through pages, leaving little round holes. Harmless and everywhere — your most reliable source of scrap paper.
dex.lore: The original enemy of books, and the reason the whole genre is named after it.

dex.description: A pale moth that flies in looping, unpredictable paths, nibbling the cloth and thread of bindings. Its erratic flight makes it a tricky hit.
dex.lore: Drawn to lamplight and old spines. Where a Bindmoth roosts, the covers come loose.

dex.description: A quick, wingless pest that bolts for cover the instant you approach. Rare and evasive, but worth the chase — it hoards the paste that holds books together.
dex.lore: It eats the glue right out of the spine. Catching one is half reflex, half luck.

dex.description: A living ember named for the enemy of every printed page. It spreads fire as it drifts and will scorch loot left on the ground — grab your drops fast.
dex.lore: Its name is the cruelest joke in the library. It burns wax hot enough to seal a cover, if you dare collect it.

dex.description: Half eraser, half razor. It lunges to rub out a letter you're carrying — a real threat, not just loot. Beat it for graphite and the occasional blank wildcard tile.
dex.lore: It doesn't want to hurt you. It wants to un-write you.

dex.description: A shambling, stamp-covered library book that never went home. Slow and tough, but it carries the richest haul — leather and a stack of pages.
dex.lore: Return date: long past. It roams the stacks, heavy with guilt and good materials.

spawn.note: Not part of the resource-creature pool; spawned by letterScatter + WOTD guarantee (see CLAUDE.md). Weight 0 so it isn't rolled here.

dex.description: The prize catch. A living letter, skittish and quick to flee. Capture one to add its letter to your satchel — the raw material of every word you'll spell.
dex.lore: Escaped from the books when the words came loose. Round them up to set the stories right.
